jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening title sequence crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation after a mishap
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical and character-specific, though not particularly original by this point in the series

jokes.text: Beer, yes! How did you know?
jokes.setup: Mentalist claims to read drink order, something foamy starting with 'B'
jokes.punchline: Homer's amazement at the obvious guess of beer
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer gullibility - perfectly in character but predictable setup

jokes.text: Oh, they oughta call that a 'Large' Island Iced Tea.
jokes.setup: Marge orders a Long Island Iced Tea
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation based on drink size
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that fits Homer's literal-minded character

jokes.text: No, 'Long' is better.
jokes.setup: Homer suggests 'Large' Island Iced Tea would be better name
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Marge's straight-woman response - economical but unremarkable

jokes.text: [Thinking] If she doesn't like it, I'll just die!
jokes.setup: Waiter being overly invested in customer satisfaction
jokes.punchline: Dramatic internal monologue about drink approval
jokes.explanation: Nice absurdist touch with the waiter's over-dramatic internal thoughts

jokes.text: Mmm! I'd like to visit that Long Island place, if only it were real.
jokes.setup: Marge is getting drunk on Long Island Iced Tea
jokes.punchline: Her innocent belief that Long Island is fictional
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - drunk Marge's naive geography confusion is both sweet and absurd

jokes.text: I'll do it! I always end up doing it.
jokes.setup: Magician asks for volunteer
jokes.punchline: Bart's resigned acceptance of his role as the family volunteer
jokes.explanation: Self-aware meta-humor about Bart's recurring role, delivered with perfect resignation

jokes.text: That's no gentleman. That's my husband.
jokes.setup: Magician asks about the gentleman she's with
jokes.punchline: Marge's classic insult structure about Homer
jokes.explanation: Classic joke structure but well-delivered - gets rimshot and audience reaction

jokes.text: That's no gentleman. That's my husband.
jokes.setup: Magician asking about the gentleman she's with
jokes.punchline: Drunk Marge's witty putdown of Homer
jokes.explanation: Classic wife-roasting-husband joke, perfectly economical structure and gets crowd reaction

jokes.text: Wow. Mom got a laugh. / I wish she drank every day.
jokes.setup: Marge gets laugh from audience
jokes.punchline: Kids' reactions to drunk mom being funny
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest kids' perspective on their usually uptight mother being fun when drunk

jokes.text: You talk too much. Abraca-blab-ra. Am I right?
jokes.setup: Magician giving long explanation
jokes.punchline: Drunk Marge interrupts with magic word pun
jokes.explanation: Drunk Marge being uncharacteristically rude with a clever pun - quotable moment

jokes.text: Tell you what? Let's get this ball gag on ya.
jokes.setup: Marge heckling the magic act
jokes.punchline: Magician escalating to bondage equipment to shut her up
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation - magician resorting to extreme measures

jokes.text: You won't feel a thing. You'll feel four things.
jokes.setup: Magician reassuring Marge about the trick
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction about feeling four things
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke with perfect economy - classic magician dark humor

jokes.text: So she was made of chimps. Man, magic can do anything!
jokes.setup: Magic trick gone wrong with chimps attacking Marge
jokes.punchline: Bart's matter-of-fact acceptance of impossible explanation
jokes.explanation: Bart's innocent logic applied to absurd situation - shows his trusting nature toward magic

jokes.text: Gee, I wish I could, Son. But we already left the gift shop.
jokes.setup: Bart asks for magic kit from gift shop they're currently in
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical refusal based on technicality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - technically correct but absurdly applied, completely in character

jokes.text: That's a party magic college. / It is not. / Yah-huh. / Nuh-huh.
jokes.setup: Magic kit approved by Royal Magic College of Hyderabad
jokes.punchline: Lisa's snobbery about magic education standards
jokes.explanation: Lisa's intellectual snobbery extending to magic colleges - good character moment

jokes.text: Mommy needs some quiet right now.
jokes.setup: Kids arguing about magic colleges
jokes.punchline: Hungover Marge using mommy voice while clearly suffering
jokes.explanation: Good callback to drunk Marge storyline - her hangover making normal parenting difficult

jokes.text: Hmm. Mm-hmm. Hmm. Well, there's your problem.
jokes.setup: Mechanic examining car with fish problem
jokes.punchline: Professional assessment of obviously absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan humor - treating fish-in-engine as routine mechanical issue

jokes.text: Oh. Well, that's a foreign fish we're looking at. So, uh, 6,500.
jokes.setup: Car needs expensive repairs
jokes.punchline: Mechanic's absurd justification for high cost
jokes.explanation: Generic mechanic ripoff joke - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: Witness mystifying feats that will leave you scratching your head... until it's raw and bloody!
jokes.setup: Bart advertising his magic show
jokes.punchline: Escalation to disturbing imagery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal magic spiel to disturbing imagery - fits Bart's mischievous nature

jokes.text: Come on. Pony up, Flanders. The kid's not turning tricks for nothin'.
jokes.setup: Trying to get neighbor to pay for magic show
jokes.punchline: Accidentally using prostitution terminology
jokes.explanation: Dark innuendo humor - Homer's unintentional inappropriate phrasing

jokes.text: Yeah, you know: Magic, fortune telling, Oriental cooking.
jokes.setup: Flanders mentions black arts
jokes.punchline: Homer's random inclusion of Oriental cooking
jokes.explanation: Homer's random association logic - the Oriental cooking addition is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Dad, only one of them is real. / I know, but which one?
jokes.setup: Confronted by street performers/thugs
jokes.punchline: Homer can't tell which twin is real
jokes.explanation: Homer's confusion about obvious situation - good character moment but not exceptional

jokes.text: Yeah. And a good dad wouldn't miss his son's little league games. / I told you! I find them boring!
jokes.setup: Father-son guilt exchange about being good to each other
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt honesty about finding games boring
jokes.explanation: Homer's brutal honesty about parenting - uncomfortable but true to character

jokes.text: Well, I showed up for all your stupid interventions!
jokes.setup: Argument about showing up for each other
jokes.punchline: Bart's counter about Homer's interventions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and callback - implies Homer has had multiple interventions

jokes.text: My father was a monster too.
jokes.setup: Bart abandoned by Homer, looking pathetic
jokes.punchline: Old lady's casual admission about her own father
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually - good economy and timing

jokes.text: But wouldn't that make us con artists? / Well, yeah.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning the ethics of their scheme
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest acceptance of criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character work - Homer's casual acceptance of criminality

jokes.text: But God conned me out of 6,500 bucks in car repairs. So, in a way, we'd just be balancing out the universe.
jokes.setup: Bart worried about being con artists
jokes.punchline: Homer's logic about balancing cosmic justice
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted moral reasoning - blaming God for car repairs to justify conning people

jokes.text: There you go! We'd be stealing from people we know. It's just like the seasons.
jokes.setup: Continuing justification for conning people
jokes.punchline: Homer's nonsensical comparison to seasons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant non-sequitur - Homer's confidence in completely illogical comparison is perfect

jokes.text: You want to eat my fat? / I think you know the answer to that.
jokes.setup: Bart offering Homer his steak fat
jokes.punchline: Homer's inevitable yes delivered as if it's obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his gluttony is so established that the answer is obvious

jokes.text: There's the Pigeon Drop, the Ear Wigger, the Brillstein grab-
jokes.setup: Homer reading about classic cons
jokes.punchline: Mix of real-sounding and absurd con names
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor - mix of plausible and ridiculous con names

jokes.text: What are you doing? Why are you frosting that old throw pillow?
jokes.setup: Marge walking in on Homer and Bart's con preparation
jokes.punchline: Catching them making fake cake with household items
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy setup - obvious con preparation caught by innocent party

jokes.text: I could ask you the very same question. / Uh, should I just back out of the room? / Would you?
jokes.setup: Marge asks why Homer is frosting a pillow
jokes.punchline: Homer's deflection and Marge's awkward retreat
jokes.explanation: Good awkward family moment - Marge recognizing she doesn't want to know

jokes.text: Nice use of the lingo, Homer. / 10-4, kemosabe.
jokes.setup: Bart compliments Homer's con artist terminology
jokes.punchline: Homer mixing up radio codes with Lone Ranger reference
jokes.explanation: Homer's confused mixing of different vernaculars - typical character mistake

jokes.text: You'll work off that cake in the acid mines!
jokes.setup: Threatening punishment for cake destruction
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous industrial punishment for minor accident
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation - 'acid mines' is perfectly ridiculous threat

jokes.text: Well, you're the mark... of integrity.
jokes.setup: Homer calling someone a mark in con artist terms
jokes.punchline: Awkward save by adding 'of integrity'
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt to cover his slip with awkward wordplay - good character comedy

jokes.text: How'd you do your act? You left your magic kit here. / Or so it would seem.
jokes.setup: Marge catching them in lie about magic act
jokes.punchline: Bart's mysterious non-explanation
jokes.explanation: Bart's attempt at magician-like mystery to cover lying - good deflection technique

jokes.text: How'd you do your act? You left your magic kit here. / Or so it would seem.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out logical inconsistency
jokes.punchline: Homer's attempted mysterious deflection
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at mystique falls flat but stays in character

jokes.text: Is that a Long Island Iced Tea? / Oh, this? Hmm. I think it is.
jokes.setup: Lisa noticing Marge drinking again
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual denial about obviously continuing to drink
jokes.explanation: Callback to drinking subplot - Marge's casual relationship with alcohol continues

jokes.text: Mmm. You're a pretty girl.
jokes.setup: Marge getting drunk on Long Island Iced Tea
jokes.punchline: Drunk Marge complimenting Lisa
jokes.explanation: Drunk Marge being inappropriate with her daughter - uncomfortable but in character

jokes.text: Or if not him, a similar dog.
jokes.setup: Bart desperately wanting his lost dog back
jokes.punchline: Immediate willingness to accept substitute
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Bart's con artist pragmatism overriding emotion

jokes.text: Oy! I'll never be a man.
jokes.setup: Bar mitzvah cake gets destroyed in con
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's despair about not becoming a man
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse moment - his neurotic despair over religious milestone

jokes.text: Like my bike that was stolen in third grade. Plus the baldness.
jokes.setup: Homer justifying continued conning for cosmic balance
jokes.punchline: Escalation from specific incident to general life complaint
jokes.explanation: Homer's expanding list of grievances against the universe - good character logic

jokes.text: Wait a minute. This seems an awful lot like that movie Paper Moon. / Run, Dad!
jokes.setup: Bible con being performed on Ned Flanders
jokes.punchline: Ned recognizing the con from movie reference
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor - Ned's movie knowledge saving him from con is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Dad? / That's right. / You don't smoke a pipe. / That's right.
jokes.setup: Grampa appearing mysteriously with pipe
jokes.punchline: Immediate acknowledgment that the prop doesn't fit
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag acknowledgment - calling out the obvious costume choice

jokes.text: They used to call me Grifty McGrift. I wrote the book on flimflammin'.
jokes.setup: Grampa revealing his con artist past
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous nickname and literal book writing claim
jokes.explanation: Great Grampa moment - absurd nickname fits his character's tendency to embellish

jokes.text: In the Depression, you had to grift. Either that or work.
jokes.setup: Explaining Depression-era survival
jokes.punchline: Presenting honest work as equally undesirable alternative
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor - Grampa's warped Depression values presented matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Yeah. In the Depression, you had to grift. Either that or work.
jokes.setup: Explaining necessity of grifting in Depression
jokes.punchline: Work presented as equally undesirable alternative
jokes.explanation: Excellent Depression-era humor - work as equally bad option is perfectly cynical

jokes.text: Oh, why not? If we don't take their money, they'll just give it to some televangelist.
jokes.setup: Questioning whether to con elderly people
jokes.punchline: Homer's justification about televangelists
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted moral reasoning - comparing themselves favorably to televangelists

jokes.text: Let's dust a few fossils.
jokes.setup: Planning to con elderly people
jokes.punchline: Criminal slang mixed with age-related pun
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay - 'dust' works as both con slang and reference to elderly

jokes.text: Now this scam was in The Sting, Part Two, so nobody knows about it.
jokes.setup: Grampa explaining their con's origin
jokes.punchline: Reference to obscure sequel nobody saw
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about forgotten sequels - explains why con would work

jokes.text: I can finally afford a young, crazy, stripper wife.
jokes.setup: Grampa believing he won $10 million
jokes.punchline: His immediate plan for the money
jokes.explanation: Grampa's inappropriate elderly desires - in character but predictable

jokes.text: Yeah. I'm gonna get me the craziest, 'strippiest'-
jokes.setup: Grampa continuing inappropriate fantasy
jokes.punchline: Cut off by apparent heart attack
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation - excitement literally killing elderly character

jokes.text: Call me mint jelly, 'cause I'm on the lam!
jokes.setup: Grampa escaping from fake FBI raid
jokes.punchline: Absurd food pun for being on the run
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd pun - mint jelly/lamb wordplay is original and quotable

jokes.text: Well, this is about the flashiest car I've ever impounded.
jokes.setup: Taking Homer's beat-up car
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about car's poor condition
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup - contrasting official language with obvious reality

jokes.text: Yeah. We're not criminals. We're just two crazy, mixed-up kids.
jokes.setup: Homer pleading with fake FBI agent
jokes.punchline: Adult man calling himself and son 'kids'
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish pleading - fits character but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, sure. I'll put you in the Rick James suite. It's super freaky.
jokes.setup: Homer asking to show Bart a jail cell
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's Rick James reference with song lyric
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's pop culture reference with song lyric - good character moment

jokes.text: That's not an F.B.I. Badge. / 'Colgate Cavity Patrol'? Ohh!
jokes.setup: Realizing they were conned by fake FBI agent
jokes.punchline: Badge is actually from dental hygiene promotion
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag payoff - dental badge perfectly absurd substitute for FBI

jokes.text: Well, lies got us into this mess, Son. From now on, it's honesty all the way.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring they should be honest from now on
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to them lying to Marge
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic structure - promise of honesty immediately broken

jokes.text: You were carjacked in the church parking lot? / Absolutely. We had stopped in for a quick prayer
jokes.setup: Homer lying about being carjacked
jokes.punchline: Adding religious detail to make lie more believable
jokes.explanation: Homer's elaborate lying with religious touches - good character development

jokes.text: He was foreign, and he had- Wild, bushy hair, like an animal.
jokes.setup: Describing fictional carjacker
jokes.punchline: Accidentally describing Groundskeeper Willie perfectly
jokes.explanation: Good setup for next plot development - accidentally describing Willie

jokes.text: Uh, well, he seemed like a loner. Kept to himself mostly.
jokes.setup: Homer describing fictional carjacker
jokes.punchline: Contradictory description of loner who kept to himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's redundant criminal description - shows his poor lying skills

jokes.text: I did not do it, I tell ya!
jokes.setup: Willie being arrested for carjacking
jokes.punchline: Standard innocent defendant protestation
jokes.explanation: Standard wrongful arrest moment - Willie's predictable protests

jokes.text: At least, let me comb me hair. It's all wild and bushy. / What do you care, Loner.
jokes.setup: Willie being arrested for Homer's fake carjacking description
jokes.punchline: Willie matching Homer's description perfectly
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback - Willie perfectly matching Homer's made-up description by coincidence

jokes.text: Hmm. She hasn't touched her Manwich.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Maggie being in safe house
jokes.punchline: Lisa's concern about Maggie's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Lisa's motherly concern in absurd situation - good character consistency

jokes.text: It's 10:00 in the morning. / I guess you haven't heard of chloroform.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting they go to bed at 10 AM
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual reference to drugging the family
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about drugging family - shocking casual delivery makes it work

jokes.text: I guess you haven't heard of chloroform.
jokes.setup: Bart questioning morning bedtime
jokes.punchline: Homer threatening to drug his son to avoid guilt
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation - Homer's extreme measures to avoid consequences

jokes.text: Carjacking? Who said anything about a carjacking?
jokes.setup: Willie mentions carjacking in his defense
jokes.punchline: Prosecutor's trap question
jokes.explanation: Classic courtroom trap - good structure and timing for legal comedy

jokes.text: Fuzzy like Willie's beard? / Yes! Exactly! No, no. I mean the whole incident is hazy. / Hazy like the moors of Scotland?
jokes.setup: Homer's memory being fuzzy
jokes.punchline: Prosecutor's leading questions making Homer incriminate Willie
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - prosecutor expertly manipulating Homer's testimony

jokes.text: Yes! Exactly! No, no. I mean the whole incident is hazy.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor's leading question
jokes.punchline: Homer accidentally agreeing then trying to backtrack
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer testimony disaster - agreeing before thinking

jokes.text: Hazy like the moors of Scotland?
jokes.setup: Homer saying incident was hazy
jokes.punchline: Prosecutor connecting to Willie's heritage
jokes.explanation: Excellent prosecutorial wordplay - connecting everything to Willie

jokes.text: I believe we was promised $5.00 a day.
jokes.setup: Judge asking for jury verdict
jokes.punchline: Jury focused on payment instead of justice
jokes.explanation: Good satire of jury system - financial motivation over justice

jokes.text: I believe we was promised $5.00 a day. / All right then. Guilty!
jokes.setup: Jury foreman asked for verdict
jokes.punchline: Immediate guilty verdict based on payment complaint
jokes.explanation: Great absurd logic - jury more concerned with payment than justice

jokes.text: Ten years? But I 'didna' do it!
jokes.setup: Willie receiving harsh sentence
jokes.punchline: Dialectical protest of innocence
jokes.explanation: Standard Willie character moment - dialect and continued protests

jokes.text: Let's see where he's going with this.
jokes.setup: Willie grabbing gun and taking hostage
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual interest in dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction - treating dangerous situation like entertainment

jokes.text: Hey, watch it! / I look like cable TV.
jokes.setup: Skinner with fake blood pack and squibs
jokes.punchline: His complaint about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Skinner's vanity about his appearance even in fake blood - mild character moment

jokes.text: Devon Bradley. Character actor, dancer, singer. I'm a triple threat!
jokes.setup: Revealing the fake FBI agent's identity
jokes.punchline: His showbiz self-promotion
jokes.explanation: Character actor's shameless self-promotion - good one-off character moment

jokes.text: I know it seems far-fetched, even insulting to your intelligence. But there's a simple and highly satisfying explanation. You see-
jokes.setup: Lisa about to explain the elaborate setup
jokes.punchline: Interrupted by surf's up announcement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the absurd plot followed by perfect interruption - self-aware and satisfying
